threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still cross the road when you see a bus coming, not realizing it’ll just stop for you at the next stop.
Caught a night bus home from Brixton last week—two guys were arguing over the best way to properly pronounce 'Sainsbury's'. Classic South London.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I was home when I heard a tattooed guy serenading the Kranoldplatz pigeons with an accordion at dawn last week.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a public park that doubles as a massive open-air club at night—nothing like learning to dance under the trees.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate BART delays like they’re just another Tuesday. It’s the eye-roll and shrug combo that gives it away.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus where the driver was belting out Whitney Houston. Random, but for a moment, it felt like a concert on wheels. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: You know that fancy Gaudí house? Most locals avoid it like the plague. We’d rather wander the side streets for real tapas and local chatter.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still stand awkwardly at the bus stop, consulting their phones while locals seamlessly navigate the timetable boards.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the buses will take you anywhere on time. Bless, wait till you see the 48 at rush hour!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw an old man in a tracksuit dancing with a broom outside his Kiezkiosk, like it was just another sunny day.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a Döner at 3 AM and hear a spontaneous techno party popping off in the park. It's a wild harmony of late-night cravings and Berlin beats.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they nonchalantly side-step the city's infamous dog poop mines while chatting on their phone.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy playing bagpipes in the back of a crowded 14-Muni just as we hit the tunnel. Instant mood shift, like a live concert in a moving box.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all bars close at 1 AM. In Gràcia, it's hard to find a place that shuts before 3 AM, especially on weekends.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll catch them staring at the metro map like it's a puzzle, while locals navigate in a blur. It's the telltale sign of a fresh face.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it Dalston Junction; locals just say 'the Junction' like it's the only one that matters. It’s all in the tone, mate.
